Our Mission: Restoring the Earth, One Initiative at a Time

Founded in 2015, Environmental Public Awareness began as a small collective of dedicated advocates in Seattle. What started as a local effort to document urban deforestation has evolved into a global movement for ecological restoration and public education. We believe that awareness is the first step toward action, and our history is defined by a commitment to transparency and sustainable solutions. Through rigorous research and community engagement, we work tirelessly to bridge the gap between environmental science and public policy, ensuring a resilient future for all.

SUSTAINABLE LIVING • ECOSYSTEM RESTORATION • PLASTIC REDUCTION • RENEWABLE ENERGY • WILDLIFE PROTECTION • SUSTAINABLE LIVING • ECOSYSTEM RESTORATION • PLASTIC REDUCTION • RENEWABLE ENERGY • WILDLIFE PROTECTION

Our Environmental Initiatives

Urban Forest Restoration

We are dedicated to expanding green spaces in urban centers through strategic tree planting and native species preservation.

Learn More
Zero-Waste Campaigns

Empowering communities with educational workshops and resources to drastically reduce household waste and plastic consumption.

Learn More
Renewable Energy Advocacy

Advocating for clean energy transitions and providing technical support for households looking to go 100% solar.

Learn More

Join Our Movement

We believe that collective action is the key to a sustainable future. Here are several ways you can get involved:

Volunteer with us at local restoration sites or educational workshops.

Spread Awareness by sharing our initiatives on social media or in your community.

Donate to support our research and outreach programs for environmental justice.

Get Involved Now

Community Voices

"The initiatives we've seen in Seattle are not just about trees; they are about restoring the very heartbeat of our city."

Sarah Jenkins, Urban Ecologist

"Public awareness is the first step toward collective action. We are seeing a shift in how our community engages with nature."

Marcus Thorne, Community Lead

"Environmental advocacy should be accessible to everyone. This group has made it possible for us to take meaningful action."

Elena Rodriguez, Local Resident
